insights.impact: Voucher payments represent only 34 orders (0.7% of volume) and R$ 126 of total payment value — effectively negligible — suggesting the voucher channel has collapsed or was never properly activated, leaving a known conversion lever unused.

SP alone accounts for 28% of all orders (1,331) and R$ 6,386 of total revenue — a single-state dependency means any regional disruption (logistics, regulation, macro) disproportionately hits the national topline.

The top 3 states (SP, RJ, MG) account for 50.5% of all orders (2,382 of 4,725) — heavy southeast concentration limits growth ceiling and signals under-penetration of BA, PR, RS and other mid-tier states that have non-trivial volumes.

Voucher orders carry an average basket of R$ 37.07 vs R$ 50.89 for credit_card — a 27% AOV gap means voucher-heavy promotions, if activated, would dilute blended AOV unless minimum spend thresholds are enforced.
